rules_status.notes: is_htmx(request: HttpRequest) → isHtmx(headers: IncomingHttpHeaders). Signature adaptation: the function now accepts the headers map directly instead of the full Django request object — a valid and more testable design that does NOT alter the business semantics. Header name 'HX-Request' → 'hx-request' (lowercase) is technically correct: Node.js normalises all incoming HTTP headers to lowercase on ingress (RFC 7230 §3.2), whereas Django's HttpRequest.headers performs a case-insensitive lookup. Both versions return true iff the header value is exactly the string 'true'. Edge case (absent header or value ≠ 'true') verified: Python returns False (missing key in get()), TypeScript returns false (undefined === 'true' is false). Semantics are identical.

clean_integrity_error(error: Exception) -> str | None → parseSequelizeError(error: unknown): string | null. Both functions share an identical public contract: take an error, return the offending field name (e.g. 'email', 'username') or null if the error is not a recognised unique-constraint violation. The Python source manually parses raw driver messages from two backends (psycopg2.errors.UniqueViolation via string-split ':' then '(' then ')'; sqlite3.IntegrityError via string-split ': ' then '.'). Sequelize already performs this parsing for both backends and exposes the result as error.errors[0].path on UniqueConstraintError, making the translation simpler without altering the contract. Covered cases verified: (1) email uniqueness violation → 'email', (2) username uniqueness violation → 'username', (3) non-unique-constraint error → null, (4) malformed/empty errors → null. Catch-all silent-failure behaviour is preserved via the outer try/catch block. The optional-chaining path (error.errors[0]?.path ?? null) correctly returns null when errors[] is empty, matching the Python source's behaviour for malformed messages.

conduit_context(request) — a Django SSR context processor that exposes user data ({username, email, bio, image}) as JSON in every template rendering context — has NO equivalent in target/backend/src/shared/. This is a documented deferral: translation_log.json (agent 18) explicitly states 'action_required: Implement in Angular AuthService (accounts/templates sprint)'. The rule is architecturally impossible to implement in the backend shared module because: (1) Django context processors are an SSR-only mechanism, (2) the Angular SPA exposes current user reactively via AuthService/AuthStore rather than injecting it into server-rendered HTML, (3) the bio/image empty-string-to-null normalisation edge case of BR-003 must be handled in the Angular AuthService mapping layer. This deferral constitutes 'acceptation explicite documentee' per migration rules — it is NOT a blocking missing rule for the helpers module review. Tracking: must be implemented in the accounts or templates sprint before Phase 5 audit.

items.notes: Confirmed dead code by Phase 0 cartography (agent 01). No callers found in any module. Django Http404 subclass has no architectural equivalent in Fastify — routes return reply.code(404) inline. Decision documented in translation_log.json (not_migrated[1]) and Phase 3 HV-001 checkpoint.

Confirmed dead code by Phase 0 cartography. Wraps Django's get_object_or_404 with ResourceNotFound — which is itself dead code. Fastify routes use Sequelize findOne() directly. Decision documented in translation_log.json (not_migrated[1]) and Phase 3 HV-001 checkpoint.

mapping.target_test: returns field name for UniqueConstraintError on email
mapping.notes: Django IntegrityError with sqlite3 cause replaced by Sequelize UniqueConstraintError with ValidationErrorItem(path='email'). Business assertion identical: result === 'email'.

mapping.target_test: returns field name for UniqueConstraintError on username
mapping.notes: Same adaptation as email test. Business assertion identical: result === 'username'.

Python: cause is None → no isinstance branch matches → returns None. TypeScript: plain Error('some unrelated error') → instanceof UniqueConstraintError is false → returns null. Business assertion identical: result is null/None.

mapping.source_test: test_returns_none_for_malformed_message — SQLiteIntegrityError('something unexpected') → None (split raises IndexError, caught by except Exception)
mapping.target_test: returns null when errors array is empty (malformed constraint error)
mapping.notes: The idiomatic Sequelize equivalent of a 'malformed raw DB message' is a UniqueConstraintError with an empty errors[] array (no path resolvable). Python path: string.split() raises IndexError → caught → None. TypeScript path: error.errors[0]?.path === undefined → ?? null → returns null. Semantics identical (unresolvable constraint → null), test fixture is different because Sequelize handles raw message parsing internally. This is the correct adaptation.

gaps.description: No unit tests for isHtmx() in source or target
gaps.notes: helpers/tests.py intentionally scopes unit tests only to 'logic hard to exercise through the browser'. is_htmx/isHtmx is a one-line comparison covered by E2E HTMX interactions. Absence of unit test in target is consistent with source intent.

findings_summary.description: BR-003 (conduit_context user JSON) is not implemented in target/backend/src/shared/. Documented deferral to Angular AuthService (accounts/templates sprint). Not a blocker — acceptation explicite documentée in translation_log.json.
findings_summary.action: Track implementation in accounts or templates sprint. Verify Angular AuthService exposes {username, email, bio: bio||null, image: image||null} and handles the empty-string-to-null normalisation edge case before Phase 5 audit.

findings_summary.description: isHtmx() has no callers in the current target codebase. The function is retained for Strangler-Fig transition adapters during the migration window. Flagged by agent 18.
findings_summary.action: After completing migration of all HTMX endpoints (articles, accounts, comments sprints), verify whether isHtmx() has any callers. Remove the file at Phase 5 audit if unused.

findings_summary.description: parseSequelizeError relies on UniqueConstraintError.errors[0].path being populated. This holds for Sequelize ORM-raised errors but should be validated for both SQLite (local/test) and PostgreSQL (production) backends with real integration tests.
findings_summary.action: Verify with integration tests during accounts and articles sprints (both backends). The path field should be 'email' for email constraint violations and 'username' for username constraint violations.
